summary: Agent1 concludes that the Gillie’s approach involves using a temporal incision technique mainly for performing closed reductions before fixing fractured bones like those found at the zygomatic arch area when they're not stable post-reduction but still need stabilization without an external fixator being necessary initially due to its minimally invasive nature compared to other surgical approaches which might require more extensive access such as coronoal cuts etc...
